beginner.question: What does align-items do in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: align-items aligns flex items along the cross axis of the flex container.

beginner.question: What are the values of align-items?
beginner.answer: The values are stretch, flex-start, flex-end, center, baseline.

beginner.question: What is the default value of align-items?
What does align-items: stretch do?
beginner.answer: stretch stretches items to fill the full cross-axis size of the container.

beginner.question: What does align-items: flex-start do?
beginner.answer: flex-start aligns items at the start of the cross axis (top by default in row direction).

beginner.question: What does align-items: center do?
beginner.answer: center aligns items in the center of the cross axis.

beginner.question: What does align-items: flex-end do?
beginner.answer: flex-end aligns items at the end of the cross axis (bottom by default in row direction).

beginner.question: What does align-items: baseline do?
beginner.answer: baseline aligns items so their text baselines line up with each other.

beginner.question: What does the align-self property do?
beginner.answer: align-self overrides the align-items value for a specific flex item, allowing individual cross-axis alignment.

beginner.question: What is the default value of align-self?
beginner.answer: The default value is auto, which inherits the container's align-items value.

intermediate.question: How does align-items: stretch affect items without a set height?
intermediate.answer: Items stretch to fill the container's cross-axis size; items with a set height/width are not stretched.

intermediate.question: How does align-items: baseline work with different font sizes?
intermediate.answer: Items are positioned so their text baselines align, which shifts items vertically when they have different font sizes.

intermediate.question: How do align-items and justify-content work together to center content?
intermediate.answer: align-items: center (cross-axis) and justify-content: center (main-axis) together center items both ways.

intermediate.question: How does align-items behave with flex-direction: column?
intermediate.answer: In column direction, the cross axis is horizontal, so align-items controls horizontal alignment.

intermediate.question: How does align-self override align-items for individual items?
intermediate.answer: Set align-self: center on one item to center it on the cross axis, while other items follow the container's align-items.

intermediate.question: How does align-items interact with min-height constraints?
intermediate.answer: min-height on the container sets the minimum cross-axis size; stretch will expand items to at least that height.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between align-items: center and justify-content: center?
intermediate.answer: align-items: center works on the cross axis; justify-content: center works on the main axis.

intermediate.question: How does align-items work when items have different heights?
intermediate.answer: With stretch, all items expand to the tallest item's height; with flex-start/center/flex-end, items keep their natural heights.

intermediate.question: How does align-items: baseline differ between row and column direction?
intermediate.answer: In row direction, baseline aligns text baselines horizontally; in column, it aligns text baselines vertically (less common).

intermediate.question: Can align-items apply to non-flex items?
intermediate.answer: No, align-items only works within a flex or grid formatting context.

advanced.question: How does align-items: baseline work when items have nested content?
advanced.answer: The baseline is taken from the inline content of each flex item; items with different nested structures may align unpredictably.

advanced.question: How does align-items interact with the margin: auto on the cross axis?
advanced.answer: margin-top: auto or margin-bottom: auto on a flex item overrides align-items for that item, pushing it to the edge.

advanced.question: How does the safe and unsafe keywords affect align-items?
advanced.answer: safe prevents data loss by avoiding alignment that causes overflow; unsafe allows overflow for strict alignment.

advanced.question: How does align-items behave with replaced elements (images) as flex items?
advanced.answer: Images have an intrinsic aspect ratio; stretch may distort them without additional constraints like object-fit.

advanced.question: How does align-items differ from align-content?
advanced.answer: align-items aligns individual items within a flex line; align-content distributes multiple flex lines along the cross axis.

advanced.question: How does align-self: auto resolve to different values in different contexts?
advanced.answer: auto takes the parent's align-items value; if no parent has align-items set, it defaults to stretch.

advanced.question: How does the CSS Box Alignment Module define align-items in Grid vs Flexbox?
advanced.answer: Both use the same property with identical values; in Grid, it aligns items within their grid area; in Flexbox, within the flex line.

advanced.question: How does align-items interact with writing modes?
advanced.answer: In vertical writing modes, the cross axis rotates; align-items adapts to the logical block direction automatically.

advanced.question: How do you create a vertically centered row of items with different heights using align-items?
advanced.answer: Use align-items: center on the flex container to vertically center items of varying heights.

advanced.question: How does align-items: stretch behave when the container has no explicit height?
advanced.answer: The cross-axis size is determined by the tallest item, so stretch has no visible effect (items already fill the line).
